sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Freight Technologies Launches Zayren Pro with Next-Generation AI Agents
feed.summary: Freight Technologies (Fr8Tech) has launched Zayren Pro, a premium commercial version of its AI-driven logistics platform, designed to expand its subscription-based SaaS revenue. Zayren Pro moves beyond simple rate prediction to enable actionable procurement execution, offering advanced AI capabilities, unlimited query capacity, and a proprietary carrier portal for the U.S.–Mexico nearshoring corridor. This platform empowers shippers and brokers with smart procurement tools and direct execution capabilities, connecting them with verified carriers.

feed.title: Analyst Remains Bullish on Amazon, TD Cowen Raises PT From $300 to $315
feed.summary: TD Cowen has raised its price target for Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) from $300 to $315, maintaining a Buy rating, based on a positive outlook from their annual advertising buyer survey. Concurrently, Raymond James analyst Josh Beck also expects a strong Q4 2025 for Amazon, driven by holiday trends, ad checks, and AWS performance, highlighting AI as a major catalyst for the stock in 2026. The article notes Amazon's significant position in e-commerce and cloud services but suggests other AI stocks might offer greater upside with less risk.

feed.title: Autodesk layoffs today: Software company cuts 7% of jobs in latest tech downsizing
feed.summary: Autodesk announced plans to lay off about 1,000 employees, primarily in sales roles, representing approximately 7% of its workforce. This move is part of a multi-phase restructuring plan to reallocate resources towards strategic priorities. CEO Andrew Anagnost stated that these are not annual layoffs or a shift to AI replacement, and the company expects pre-tax restructuring charges between $135 and $160 million.

feed.title: Albemarle shares jump as rising lithium prices build upon efficiency gains
feed.summary: Albemarle Corp. shares have risen over 30% this year, driven by analyst predictions of increasing lithium prices and successful efficiency initiatives. Analysts from HSBC and Truist have upgraded their ratings and significantly raised price targets, reflecting optimism about Albemarle's prospects in the rebounding lithium market. The company is set to report Q4 and full-year results soon, with expectations of narrowing losses and increased revenue, supported by reduced capital spending and asset sales.

feed.title: Mixed options sentiment in Alphabet Class A with shares up 1.07%
feed.summary: Alphabet Class A (GOOGL) shares rose by 1.07% to $331.91, exhibiting mixed options sentiment. While overall options volume was average, calls outnumbered puts, and put-call skew flattened, suggesting a modestly bullish outlook. The company is set to report earnings on February 4, 2026, with option markets pricing in a 50% probability of a move greater than 5.1%.

feed.title: BMS bets on Janux’s novel solid tumour therapy through $850m deal
feed.summary: Bristol Myers Squibb (BMS) has entered into an exclusive global licensing agreement with Janux Therapeutics for a novel solid tumor therapy, valued at up to $850 million. This deal strengthens BMS's oncology pipeline as it faces upcoming patent expirations for key drugs like Opdivo and Eliquis. Janux will handle preclinical development up to IND submission, after which BMS will take over clinical development and commercialization, with Janux also receiving tiered royalties on global sales.

feed.title: Final Trades: Vertex Pharma, Kimberly-Clark, S&P Global and Exxon Mobil
feed.summary: The Investment Committee has revealed their top stock picks for the second half of the year. The report focuses on companies such as Vertex Pharma, Kimberly-Clark, S&P Global, and Exxon Mobil, providing viewers with stocks to watch. The article is primarily a video segment summary, encouraging viewers to watch the full discussion.

feed.title: Tech briefs: Deere's enhanced AI integration; new drone-based crop intel project launched
feed.summary: This article provides tech briefs on agricultural innovations. It covers Deere's advanced AI integration in its operations and the launch of a new drone-based crop intelligence project.

feed.title: Colgate-Palmolive & WHO Foundation: Oral Health Education
feed.summary: Colgate-Palmolive has partnered with the WHO Foundation in a multi-year funding agreement to advance oral health education and awareness. This collaboration aims to elevate oral health as a public health priority, support its integration into national health systems, and expand preventive measures. The initiative, announced at WEF's Annual Meeting, focuses on scaling education, guiding policy, and increasing prioritization of oral health globally.

feed.title: Tractor Supply plans new Plymouth Township location
feed.summary: Tractor Supply Company is planning a new retail store in Plymouth Township, Michigan, near a new Meijer grocery store. The township's planning commission has approved the special land use request for the 21,533-square-foot building and outdoor display area. This development is part of a growing commercial cluster along the Five Mile corridor, and commissioners emphasized the importance of preserving heritage trees and ensuring pedestrian connectivity.

feed.title: Lemonade’s Tesla insurance product validates FSD technology, Morgan Stanley notes
feed.summary: Morgan Stanley reiterated an Equalweight rating and $425.00 price target for Tesla, noting that Lemonade's new autonomous car insurance product validates Tesla's Full Self-Driving (FSD) technology. Lemonade's policy ties insurance pricing to FSD miles driven, offering up to 50% rate reductions, which incentivizes FSD usage and signals the insurer's recognition of improved accident risk during autonomous operation. This development suggests that insurance could become a market-based validator for autonomous driving advancements.

feed.title: Josh Brown's "best stocks in the market:" First Solar
feed.summary: Josh Brown, CEO of Ritholtz Wealth Management, appeared on CNBC's "Halftime Report" to discuss his "best stocks in the market." He specifically spotlighted First Solar (FSLR) during his segment. The article provides only this brief mention and does not offer further details from his analysis.

feed.title: Target Stacks Its Board With Star Executives From Nike, HanesBrands
feed.summary: Target Corporation has appointed John Hoke, III, a longtime Nike design leader, and Steve Bratspies, former CEO of HanesBrands, to its board of directors. These appointments are part of Target's strategy to enhance innovation and increase shareholder value as it enters a new growth phase with CEO Michael Fiddelke. The company also declared a quarterly dividend of $1.14 per common share, payable on March 1, 2026.

feed.title: Smart Money Is Betting Big In CSGP Options
feed.summary: Benzinga's options scanner detected over 12 options trades for CoStar Group (NASDAQ: CSGP) totaling $1,473,080, with significant investors targeting a price range of $40.0 to $95.0 over the next three months. The stock is currently trading at $67.55, up 4.29%, with analysts having an average target price of $72.0.

feed.title: A Closer Look at UnitedHealth Group's Options Market Dynamics
feed.summary: Financial giants have shown significant bullish interest in UnitedHealth Group (NYSE: UNH) through the options market, with 91 unusual trades observed. While 47% of traders were bullish and 29% bearish, call options accounted for a higher value ($4,094,690) compared to puts ($2,405,589). The analysis indicates a targeted price range of $200.0 to $560.0 for UNH during the last quarter, with current trading showing a 1.82% price increase to $354.07 and the stock potentially approaching overbought conditions.

feed.title: Intel, GE Aerospace, Moderna, Abbott, Arista, Mobileye, McCormick, and More Movers
feed.summary: Stocks were on the rise Thursday following President Donald Trump's announcement of a "framework of a future deal" with NATO concerning Greenland. Equities maintained their gains after new data revealed that the Federal Reserve's preferred inflation gauge largely met expectations in November. The article lists Intel, GE Aerospace, Moderna, Abbott, Arista Networks, Mobileye, and McCormick as some of the day's movers.

feed.title: Is GM Set for 14th Straight EPS Beat in Q4? How to Play the Stock Now
feed.summary: General Motors (GM) is expected to report its fourth-quarter 2025 earnings on January 27, with analysts predicting a 14th consecutive EPS beat. Despite a year-over-year decline in U.S. vehicle deliveries and EV sales in Q4 2025, GM maintained its position as the top-selling automaker in the U.S. for the full year and achieved profitability in China. The company's strategic shift away from high-volume EV production, focus on high-margin vehicles, growing software business, and strong fundamentals suggest potential for further stock upside despite recording $6 billion in special charges for EV rollback.

feed.title: eGain Announces Solve 26 London: Premier Conference on AI-Powered Knowledge Management for Customer Experience Automation
feed.summary: eGain Corporation announced its Solve 26 London conference, a premier event focusing on AI-powered knowledge management for customer experience automation. The conference, scheduled for May 6-7, 2026, aims to address the "AI productivity paradox" by showcasing how trusted, AI-powered knowledge can drive measurable business outcomes. Attendees will hear customer success stories from Global 2000 companies, learn about eGain's product innovations, and participate in hands-on workshops.

feed.title: Genesco Amends Credit Agreement to Extend Maturity, Cut Costs
feed.summary: Genesco (GCO) announced a fourth amendment to its credit agreement, extending the maturity of its revolving credit facility to January 16, 2031, while maintaining current borrowing bases and collateral. This amendment relaxes financial covenants, shifts Canadian rates to Term CORRA, removes credit spread adjustments for domestic borrowings, and revises pricing, aiming to improve funding flexibility and lower financing costs. Spark's AI Analyst gives GCO a "Neutral" rating, citing strong revenue growth and free cash flow balanced by weak profitability.

feed.title: The Quiet Powerhouse: A Deep-Dive into Jack Henry & Associates (JKHY)
feed.summary: Jack Henry & Associates (JKHY) is presented as a "quiet powerhouse" in the American financial infrastructure, providing technology for over 7,500 community banks and credit unions. The article details its resilient business model with high recurring revenue, its strong financial performance, and its strategic transition to cloud-native architecture. JKHY's focus on service and its niche in the community banking sector are highlighted as key competitive advantages.

feed.title: Keysight Technologies (NYSE:KEYS) Sets New 52-Week High - Still a Buy?
feed.summary: Keysight Technologies (NYSE:KEYS) recently hit a new 52-week high of $220.12, with analysts maintaining a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating and a target price of $219.82. The company surpassed Q1 earnings expectations and announced a $1.5 billion share repurchase plan. Despite strong institutional ownership, insiders have been net sellers in the last quarter.

feed.title: TE Connectivity PLC (NYSE:TEL) Achieves Record Orders
feed.summary: TE Connectivity PLC (NYSE:TEL) reported record orders of $5.1 billion, a 28% year-over-year increase, driving its stock up 1.32%. The company exceeded analyst expectations with adjusted EPS of $2.72 and revenue of $4.67 billion in Q1 FY26, attributing growth to strategic investments in AI, energy grid modernization, and advanced vehicle technologies, particularly within its Industrial and Transportation segments. Despite macroeconomic uncertainties and rising metal prices, the company projects continued revenue growth and adjusted EPS of $2.65 for the current quarter.

feed.title: Teledyne Technologies Inc (NYSE:TDY) Exceeds Analyst Expectations
feed.summary: Teledyne Technologies Inc (NYSE:TDY) exceeded analyst expectations for Q4 FY25 earnings and revenue, driven by strong defense sales and a recovery in commercial businesses. The company reported adjusted EPS of $6.30 on revenues of $1.61 billion and provided optimistic full-year 2026 adjusted earnings guidance of $23.45 to $23.85 per share. Teledyne also continued its growth strategy through acquisitions, recently acquiring DD-Scientific.

feed.title: Procter & Gamble acquires Austin-based Wonderbelly
feed.summary: Austin-based digestive aid company Wonderbelly has been acquired by Procter & Gamble less than five years after its founding. Wonderbelly will now join P&G's portfolio of household brands, including Dawn, Bounty, Tide, and Pampers. This acquisition highlights the growing trend of major brands investing in regional food and beverage companies.

feed.title: First Foundation (NASDAQ:FFWM) Reaches New 12-Month High - Should You Buy?
feed.summary: First Foundation (NASDAQ:FFWM) recently hit a new 52-week high of $6.47, closing at $6.4450 with significant trading volume. This surge follows analyst upgrades, including an "overweight" rating from Stephens and a $6.75 price target from Piper Sandler, contributing to a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating. The company, a bank holding firm specializing in banking and wealth management for high-net-worth individuals and businesses, has a market cap of $536.59 million and high institutional ownership.

feed.title: Trade Tracker: Karen Firestone sells Intuit and Booking Holdings
feed.summary: Karen Firestone, Executive Chairman of Aureus Asset Management, discussed her latest portfolio adjustments on CNBC's "Halftime Report." During the segment, she explained her decision to sell positions in Intuit (INTU) and Booking Holdings (BKNG). The article highlights her recent investment moves as part of the "Trade Tracker" series.

feed.title: Moody’s changes News Corporation’s outlook to positive, affirms Baa3 By Investing.com
feed.summary: Moody's Ratings has upgraded News Corporation's outlook from stable to positive while affirming its Baa3 senior unsecured notes ratings, driven by the company's significant debt reduction. News Corp's debt-to-EBITDA ratio improved to 2.5x for the 12 months ending September 30, 2025, supported by strong revenue in its Dow Jones and Digital Real Estate Services segments. Moody's anticipates continued strong operating performance, potentially leading to a ratings upgrade within 12-18 months if conservative financial policies are maintained and debt-to-EBITDA stays below 2.5x.

feed.title: Amgen (AMGN) expands obesity strategy with Ro collaboration on GLP-1 therapies
feed.summary: Amgen has partnered with Ro to launch a new program providing patients with access to Amgen's GLP-1 therapies for obesity, if approved. This collaboration aims to offer comprehensive care, including medication, coaching, and support, following a doctor's consultation. Ro's Body Program currently serves patients access to Novo Nordisk's Wegovy and Zepbound.

feed.title: Italy Tractor Market Research Report 2025-2030 Featuring Prominent Vendors - CNH Industrial, Deere & Co, AGCO, Kubota, SDF Group, and TAFE - ResearchAndMarkets.com
feed.summary: The Italy Tractor Market is projected to grow from 15,448 units in 2024 to 16,589 units by 2030, at a CAGR of 1.19%. Prominent vendors include CNH Industrial, SDF Group, AGCO Corporation, Kubota, and Deere & Company, with significant market share. The market is driven by government policies supporting organic farming and credit, while facing restraints from agricultural credit crunch and climate variability.

feed.title: GM plans to move Buick Envision production from China to U.S. in 2028
feed.summary: General Motors announced plans to shift production of the next-generation Buick Envision from China to the U.S. in 2028, moving it to the Fairfax Assembly plant in Kansas. This decision aims to strengthen GM's domestic manufacturing footprint and support U.S. jobs, following auto import tariffs and regulatory shifts. While the Envision will be assembled in Kansas for the North American market, production in China may continue for other international markets, and GM will continue shipping Envisions from China until the U.S. production begins.

feed.title: Royal Caribbean Group (RCL) Valuation Check After Fortune Admired List Recognition And New Miami Terminal Project
feed.summary: Royal Caribbean Group (RCL) has been recognized on Fortune's World’s Most Admired Companies 2026 list and is undertaking new projects like a LEED-focused terminal at PortMiami. Despite a 19.13% total shareholder return over the past year, the stock has seen recent declines, leading to a valuation check. The article suggests RCL is undervalued based on its P/E ratio of 18.6x compared to peers and a discounted cash flow model showing a 51.4% discount to estimated future value.

feed.title: Jack in the Box and The Hundreds Launch Limited Edition Collection Celebrating 75 Years
feed.summary: Jack in the Box is collaborating with The Hundreds to release a limited edition "Jack Was Here!" collection starting January 28, 2026, to celebrate Jack's 75th anniversary. This partnership aims to blend street culture, food, and fashion, strengthening brand visibility and attracting younger consumers. The collection will be available through four drops throughout the year, with early access for subscribers and members.

feed.title: 10 Materials Stocks With Whale Alerts In Today's Session
feed.summary: This article identifies 10 materials stocks that experienced significant options activity, known as "whale alerts," during today's trading session. These alerts, tracked by Benzinga's options activity scanner, indicate large institutional trades that could signal potential trading opportunities or market sentiment shifts. The report details specific put and call options trades, their sentiment (bullish, bearish, or neutral), expiration dates, strike prices, and total trade value for companies like Albemarle, Barrick Mining, and The Mosaic Co.

feed.title: Fulton Financial stock hits 52-week high at $21.10
feed.summary: Fulton Financial Corporation's stock reached a new 52-week high of $21.10, indicating a positive trajectory and investor confidence with a 2.24% 1-year change. This surge follows the company's strong fourth-quarter financial results, which exceeded analyst expectations with adjusted earnings of $0.55 per diluted share and closely aligned revenue of $336.02 million. The bank also reported an increased net interest income of $266 million and maintained a stable net interest margin of 3.59%.

feed.title: McDonald’s bets big on hot honey and protein to bring in customers
feed.summary: McDonald's is expanding its menu with new hot honey sauce offerings and protein-focused sandwiches, including the Hot Honey Sausage Egg Biscuit and Bacon Hot Honey McCrispy Sandwich, starting January 27. These additions are part of a broader strategy to attract customers and increase traffic by focusing on emerging food trends and continuing to emphasize value. The chain's renewed focus on value has intensified competition within the fast-food industry, prompting rivals to introduce their own discount offerings.

feed.title: Iperionx Limited Receives USD 0.3 Million Prototype Purchase Order from American Rheinmetall
feed.summary: IperionX Limited secured a USD 0.3 million prototype purchase order from American Rheinmetall to produce 700 lightweight titanium components for U.S. Army heavy ground combat systems. These components, manufactured in the U.S. using 100% recycled titanium via IperionX's patented HAMR and HSPT technologies, aim to significantly reduce vehicle weight by 40-45% per component. This initiative supports U.S. Government efforts to reshore critical materials supply chains and enhance domestic manufacturing, potentially leading to a larger future agreement.

feed.title: Janux Therapeutics, Inc. Announces Collaboration and Exclusive Worldwide License Agreement with Bristol Myers Squibb to Develop A Novel Tumor-Activated Therapeutic for Solid Tumors
feed.summary: Janux Therapeutics, Inc. has announced an exclusive worldwide license agreement and collaboration with Bristol Myers Squibb to develop a novel tumor-activated therapeutic for solid tumors. Under the agreement, Janux will conduct preclinical development up to IND submission, after which Bristol Myers Squibb will handle further development and global commercialization. Janux stands to receive up to $50 million in upfront and near-term milestone payments, with potential additional development, regulatory, and commercial milestones totaling approximately $800 million, alongside tiered royalties on product sales.

feed.title: First Hawaiian stock hits 52-week high at 28.29 USD
feed.summary: First Hawaiian Inc. (FHB) stock recently hit a 52-week high of $28.29, reflecting strong investor confidence and a positive market perception. The company has shown a 9.86% one-year price return and an impressive 10.92% gain over the last six months, with a P/E ratio of 13.77 and a 3.75% dividend yield suggesting it may be undervalued. This performance is supported by strong Q3 2025 earnings, which surpassed analyst expectations, and positive analyst upgrades from Barclays, TD Cowen, and Keefe, Bruyette & Woods.

feed.title: Horizon Kinetics buys Texas Pacific Land (TPL) share at $343
feed.summary: Horizon Kinetics Asset Management LLC, a significant shareholder in Texas Pacific Land Corp (NYSE:TPL), recently acquired 1 share of common stock for $343.48 on January 21, 2026. This purchase increases Horizon Kinetics' direct ownership to 3,487,696 shares. The transaction follows a period of several developments for Texas Pacific Land, including a Q3 2025 earnings miss, a strategic partnership with Bolt Data & Energy, a three-for-one stock split, and new "Overweight" analyst coverage from KeyBanc.

feed.title: General Motors (GM) Plans Shift of Buick Envision Production to Kansas
feed.summary: General Motors (GM) plans to move the production of its future-generation Buick Envision compact SUV from China to Kansas by 2028, largely due to U.S. tariffs. This shift also includes discontinuing the production of the all-electric Chevrolet Bolt in China within the next 18 months, indicating a change in GM's EV strategy. While GM shows strong revenue growth, its profitability and valuation metrics present a mixed financial picture, with an Altman Z-Score suggesting potential financial challenges.

feed.title: NextEra Energy vs. Southern Company: Which Is a Better Utility Pick?
feed.summary: This article compares NextEra Energy (NEE) and Southern Company (SO), two prominent U.S. electric utilities, to determine which presents a better investment opportunity. Both companies are actively investing in clean energy and offer stable cash flows and reliable dividends. While closely matched in many metrics, NextEra Energy received a Zacks Rank #2 (Buy) due to better earnings growth projections, lower debt, and superior recent price performance, giving it a slight edge over Southern Company, which has a Zacks Rank #4 (Sell).

feed.title: Hecate Energy Group to Become Public Company Through Business Combination with EGH Acquisition Corp.
feed.summary: Hecate Energy Group LLC, an independent energy infrastructure developer, announced a definitive business combination agreement with EGH Acquisition Corp. This agreement will result in Hecate becoming a public company, listed on Nasdaq under the ticker symbol "HCTE". The announcement was made from Chicago on January 22, 2026.

feed.title: Arista Networks rips higher amid jump in call buying
feed.summary: Arista Networks experienced a significant stock surge, marking its best day of the new year, driven by a substantial increase in call option buying from traders. Goldman Sachs previously identified Arista as a top tactical trade, citing its strong position in data center spending and potential for a 20% rise. The company also benefits from Microsoft's expansion plans, a major client, although no direct news specific to Arista sparked the immediate rally, suggesting the options activity and broader market sentiment around data centers are key drivers.

feed.title: Trump sues JPMorgan Chase and CEO Jamie Dimon for $5B over alleged 'political' debanking
feed.summary: Donald Trump has filed a $5 billion lawsuit against JPMorgan Chase and its CEO Jamie Dimon, accusing the bank of politically motivated "debanking." The lawsuit alleges that JPMorgan Chase unilaterally closed several of Trump's and his affiliated entities' bank accounts in 2021 without warning, citing their belief that the decision was driven by a desire to distance itself from Trump's conservative political views following the January 6th protest. Trump's legal team is demanding a jury trial, claiming trade libel, violation of Florida’s unfair and deceptive trade practices act, and breach of implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing, in addition to alleging the bank placed him on an erroneous blacklist.

feed.title: Rhode Island may ditch Mr. Potato Head license plates after Hasbro moves to Boston
feed.summary: Rhode Island lawmakers are considering ending the option for Mr. Potato Head specialty license plates following Hasbro's decision to move its headquarters to Boston. Representative Brian Newberry introduced a proposal to stop offering the plates, citing Hasbro's departure as a loss of economic benefits for the state. However, the Rhode Island Community Food Bank, which receives half of the $40 plate fee, opposes the move, as it has generated nearly $60,000 for their hunger relief efforts over the years.

feed.title: Travelers Companies Inc (NYSE:TRV) Surpasses Market Expectations
feed.summary: Travelers Companies Inc (NYSE:TRV) announced better-than-expected results for the fourth quarter and full year of FY25, with its stock rising 1.31%. The company reported significant increases in net written premiums, net investment income, and underwriting gains, alongside improved combined ratios. Travelers exceeded analysts' estimates for both adjusted earnings per share and revenue, driven by strong growth across its Business Insurance and Bond & Specialty Insurance segments.

feed.title: Trump Sues JPMorgan, Dimon for $5 Billion Over Alleged Debanking
feed.summary: Donald Trump has filed a lawsuit against JPMorgan Chase & Co. and its CEO, Jamie Dimon, seeking at least $5 billion. Trump alleges that the bank ceased providing banking services to him and his businesses due to political motivations. The lawsuit claims trade libel, breach of implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing, and violation of Florida’s deceptive trade practices law, while JPMorgan denies closing accounts for political or religious reasons.

feed.title: United Airlines Earnings: Premium Seats Offset Economy Cabin Weakness
feed.summary: United Airlines Holdings (UAL) saw an 8% decrease in operating profit in Q4 2025 despite a 5% increase in revenue and capacity, as rising premium cabin sales (up 11%) were offset by a 5% drop in main cabin revenue. The company's fair value estimate has been raised to $98 from $62, reflecting its strong revenue yield, though shares appear overvalued given the unlikelihood of maintaining outsize profits indefinitely.

feed.title: Bank of America, Citigroup stocks rise on potential 10% rate credit cards
feed.summary: Bank of America (BAC) and Citigroup (C) stocks saw gains after a report indicated both banks are considering offering credit cards with a 10% interest rate cap. This move is a response to President Donald Trump's demand for such a cap, despite concerns from banking executives about potential reduced credit availability. Other financial institutions like Capital One, American Express, and Synchrony Financial also rose on the news.

feed.title: Franklin Templeton launches new ETF focused on emerging market debt (BEN:NYSE)
feed.summary: Franklin Templeton (BEN) has introduced a new actively managed exchange-traded fund, the Templeton Emerging Markets Debt ETF (NYSE Arca: TEMD). This ETF is designed to focus on emerging market debt. It aims to provide investors with a strategy for accessing emerging markets debt that stands between fully U.S. dollar-denominated options.
